title: Sibling-worktree-aware containment denial message
action: GH #31: when a session writes to a path outside its checkout, the write-guard denies with the generic 'could not be canonically contained inside the physical worktree' — technically right, humanly opaque, and observed misread as a bug. Fix: in hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs (source of truth; vendored twin auto-syncs), when a target fails containment, before emitting the generic denial check whether the resolved target lies inside a KNOWN sibling checkout: read the main store's runtime/worktree-grants.json (fail-open on any error -> generic message) and the git worktree list equivalents already derivable from resolveRoots (mainRoot, worktreesRoot). If the target is inside a granted sibling worktree, the denial instead says: this path belongs to worktree <id> (feature from its lane/state if cheaply readable, else omit) — open a session with cwd=<worktree root> to work there, or merge it back from main via bee worktree merge --id <id>; cross-tree writes stay denied. If the target is inside the MAIN checkout while the session is rooted in a worktree, say that inversely. Message change only — the deny decision itself is untouched. Tests: extend skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs (fixture now vendors the whole lib dir per wgf-1) with rows: sibling-worktree target denial names the worktree id + both remedies; unknown-outside path keeps the generic message; grants-file unreadable keeps generic (fail-open to generic, never allow). Self-onboard + render; full verify green.

truths: Cross-tree writes remain denied in every case; only the message changes.

A denial targeting a granted sibling worktree names the worktree id and both remedies (open session there / merge from main); unknown paths and any grants-read error fall back to the generic containment message.
New rows in test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs cover named-sibling, generic-fallback, and grants-unreadable; suite stays green in run_verify; hook twins byte-identical.
prohibitions: Do NOT turn any deny into an allow, and do NOT add new deny cases.
Do NOT import heavy lib modules into the hook beyond its existing dependency-light discipline (inline the grants read like adapter.mjs does).

trace.outcome: Sibling-worktree-aware containment denial message: same deny, message names the known worktree + remedies (or the inverse from a worktree-rooted session); unknown/unparseable falls back to the generic text unchanged
trace.verify_output: PASS run_verify: 51 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=33955ms (exit 0); guard-hook suite 26/26 standalone; test_render_race rerun clean (documented WSL2 flake), test_store_lock did not flake this run
